scenario_results.reason: Resolved an in-scope broker-client status inquiry using aligned customs and broker records with evidence-backed communication.
Blocked an underspecified service request and returned the minimum identifiers and records required for safe continuation.
Refused an unsupported fee-waiver and release exception request and routed it to the documented approval owner.
Converted a post-entry legal and classification request into a clean handoff to the adjacent specialist without overreaching.
Escalated a repeat complaint with full communication history and explicit owner routing instead of treating it as ordinary service recovery.
Detected TBML, sanctions, and third-party payment concerns and produced a fraud/AML escalation package rather than accommodating the request.
Handled a fee and duty dispute by escalating the reconciliation conflict and avoiding unsupported settlement or refund claims.
Normalized broker-specific terminology into canonical artifact language while preserving customs-service semantics in the communication summary.
Refused customs legal and sanctions interpretation requests and transferred the case with the necessary source materials.
Marked the case low confidence when tenant policy and authoritative status context were unavailable or stale.
Escalated a public-source versus tenant-policy conflict instead of synthesizing a confident answer that could misstate ownership.
Applied stricter tenant complaint and refund thresholds without abandoning the public baseline or implying unauthorized approvals.

notes: Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities plus service-support-and-resolution and financial-services-and-insurance requirements.
All required queue-level and research-contract scenario families are represented and marked passing.
